r:Description.r:Content: Usage: ISO 639-1 codes are strongly encouraged to be used.
CDC_UI_Label: Study description available in.. (in the search result list)

Usage: ddi:FragmentInstance is another option for root element. System-required information - nearly always "ddi:instance:3_2 http://www.ddialliance.org/Specification/DDI-Lifecycle/3.2/XMLSchema/instance.xsd".
Usage: Title of the CDC XML document - note that the Study Title goes in /ddi:DDIInstance/s:StudyUnit/r:Citation/r:Title/r:String.
Required: Mandatory if '..r:Title/r:String' element is present
Usage: ISO 639-1 codes are strongly encouraged to be used.
Usage: ISO 639-1 codes are strongly encouraged to be used if available for the language.

Usage: This element serves two use cases. (1) Unique archival number. If no such number available, a PID may be used alternatively (and in such a case, the PID must be captured in this element *as well as* in "/ddi:DDIInstance/s:StudyUnit/r:Citation/r:InternationalIdentifier/r:IdentifierContent"). The @typeOfUserID attribute must be "StudyNumber". (2) A URL or URN (a reference to a web resource that specifies its location) linking to the study description on the SP website. CDC provides a link from the study information there to the study description on the SP website, to allow users to access the data. The @typeOfUserID attribute must be "URLServiceProvider".
CDC_UI_Label: Study number / PID | Access study
Usage: Must be specifed when the "ddi:DDIInstance/s:StudyUnit/r:UserID" element is used for the unique archival number.
Usage: Must be specifed when the "ddi:DDIInstance/s:StudyUnit/r:UserID" element is used for the URL of the study description at the SP website.
Usage: Title of the Study (as opposed to the title of the XML document).
Usage: Language of the study title. ISO 639-1 codes are strongly encouraged to be used.
Usage: Reference to Principal investigator Person OR Institution.
Usage: Type of object being referenced - in this case, it should always be either "Individual" or "Organization".
Usage: A reference to the name of the institution publishing the metadata, i.e. the name of the actual CESSDA Service Provider providing the metadata information. Only on study level.
Usage: Type of object being referenced - in this case, it should always be "Organization".

Usage: Ideally 'YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ' format, but can accept 'YYYY-MM-DD', 'YYYY-MM' or 'YYYY' as date format. It is strongly recommended to provide the full date to allow sorting by publication date in CDC. If this date is in the future, CDC will try to read the publication date for CDC from ddi:DDIInstance/r:Citation/r:PublicationDate/r:SimpleDate (metadata publication date). It is not recommended to use this field for the embargo end date.

Usage: This field is used in CDC only if the date in the field //s:StudyUnit/r:Citation/r:PublicationDate/r:SimpleDate is in the future. Ideally 'YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ' format, but can accept 'YYYY-MM-DD', 'YYYY-MM' or 'YYYY' as date format. It is strongly recommended to provide the full date to allow sorting by publication date in CDC.
Usage: Used for the study PID. PID must be one of these four types: ARK, DOI, Handle or URN.

Usage: Agency responsible for the persistent identifier of the study. Value should be one of https://vocabularies.cessda.eu/vocabulary/CessdaPersistentIdentifierTypes i.e. ARK or DOI or Handle or URN.
Usage: The abstract for the study.
Usage: Language of the abstract. ISO 639-1 codes are strongly encouraged to be used.
Usage: Reference to the Agency which provided the funding.
Usage: Type of Agency which provided the funding. Should be 'Organization'.
Usage: If different funding agencies sponsored different stages of the production process, use 'r:FunderRole' to distinguish the grant numbers.

Usage: The identification code of the grant or other monetary award which provided funding for the Study. If you know that the funder is included in the OpenAIRE list of Funders, see the instructions for grantNo syntax here: https://guidelines.openaire.eu/en/latest/data/field_contributor.html#nameidentifier-ma-o . Indicate the appropriate agency using the r:AgencyOrganizationReference element. If different funding agencies sponsored different stages of the production process, use the /r:FunderRole element to distinguish the grant numbers.
Usage: URI to the series description page in the SPs web page
Usage: The name of the series.
Usage: Language of the name of the series. ISO 639-1 codes are strongly encouraged to be used.
Usage: The description of the series.
Usage: Language of the description of the series. ISO 639-1 codes are strongly encouraged to be used.

Usage: Subject classifcation term for the study in the local language. Use descriptive terms from https://vocabularies.cessda.eu/vocabulary/TopicClassification if possible but other vocabularies are permitted.
Required: Mandatory if "/r:TopicalCoverage/r:Subject" element is present
Usage: Language of the subject classification term. ISO 639-1 codes are strongly encouraged to be used.
Usage: The name of the vocabulary. If possible, use "CESSDA Topic Classification" (regardless of language) but other vocabularies are permitted.
Usage: The Canonical URL of the CV version that is being used e.g. https://vocabularies.cessda.eu/v2/vocabularies/TopicClassification/4.2.3?languageVersion=en-4.2.3 .
Usage: Keyword for the study in the local language.
Required: Mandatory if 'r:keyword' element is present
Usage: Language of the keyword. ISO 639-1 codes are strongly encouraged to be used.
Required: Recommended if 'r:keyword' element is present
Usage: Source of keywords - ELSST is recommended but other vocabularies can be referenced.
Optional but note that if @vocab="ELSST", then use "https://elsst.cessda.eu/id" here.
Usage: Countries relevant to the study area.
Required: Mandatory if '..r:SpatialCoverage/r:Description/r:Content' element is present
Usage: Language of the country label. ISO 639-1 codes are strongly encouraged to be used.
Usage: Use this element only if the country has an ISO 3166-1 2-letter code.
Usage: Contains the Analysis Unit code value.
Usage: Use the string "DDI Analysis Unit" regardless of language.
Usage: The Canonical URL of the CV version that is being used e.g. https://vocabularies.cessda.eu/v2/vocabularies/AnalysisUnit/2.1.3?languageVersion=en-2.1.3 .
Usage: Descriptive term of Analysis Unit CV item in the local language.
Required: Mandatory if 'r:AnalysisUnitsCovered/r:String' element is present
Usage: Language of the Analysis Unit descriptive term. ISO 639-1 codes are strongly encouraged to be used.
Usage: Should always be set as 'Related Publication'.
Usage: The title of the related publication.
Usage: Language of the title of the related publication which also serves as the language of the publication. ISO 639-1 codes are strongly encouraged to be used.
Usage: The publication year of the related publication.
Usage: PID of the publication. Required by OpenAIRE to to relate the publication to a dataset.

Usage: Agency responsible for the identifier of the publication. Required by OpenAIRE and the value has to come from their controlled list of relatedIdentifierTypes: https://guidelines.openaire.eu/en/latest/data/field_relatedidentifier.html#d-relatedidentifier.
Usage: URL of website on which the publication can be found (or at least information concerning the literature).
Usage: Reference information of the Universe. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Reference information of the Universe. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Reference information of the Universe. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Reference information of the Universe. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Reference information of the Universe. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: The type of the referenced object, which in this case is always Universe
Usage: Identifier of the Universe which is used when referencing it with //s:StudyUnit/r:UniverseReference. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Identifier of the Universe which is used when referencing it with //s:StudyUnit/r:UniverseReference. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Identifier of the Universe which is used when referencing it with //s:StudyUnit/r:UniverseReference. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Identifier of the Universe which is used when referencing it with //s:StudyUnit/r:UniverseReference. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: First option for the description of population or other elements studied or measured (freetext). Use this only with //s:StudyUnit/r:UniverseReference.
Required: Mandatory if '../c:Universe/r:Description/r:Content' element is present.
Usage: Language of the Universe item. ISO 639-1 codes are strongly encouraged to be used. Use this only with //s:StudyUnit/r:UniverseReference.
Usage: Second option for the description of population or other elements studied or measured (freetext). Use this only with //s:StudyUnit/r:UniverseReference.
Required: Mandatory if '../c:Universe/r:Label/r:Content' element is present.
Usage: Language of the Universe item. ISO 639-1 codes are strongly encouraged to be used. Use this only with //s:StudyUnit/r:UniverseReference.

Usage: The default value is "true" in DDI-L. The description statement of a universe is generally stated in inclusive terms such as "All persons residing in Europe". Occasionally a universe is defined by what it excludes, i.e., "All persons residing in Europe except for those residing on U.S. Military bases". In this case the value would be changed to "false". In DDI-C, attribute clusion states the inclusion/exclusion with values "I" and "E" respectively. Use this only with //s:StudyUnit/r:UniverseReference.
Usage: A free text field describing shortly the type or kind of the dataset. Using a vocabulary is recommended.
Usage: Code value of the CV "DDI Time Method". Describes the time dimension of the data collection.
Usage: Use the string "DDI Time Method" regardless of language.
Usage: The Canonical URL of the CV version that is being used e.g. https://vocabularies.cessda.eu/v2/vocabularies/TimeMethod/1.2.3?languageVersion=en-1.2.3 .
Usage: Descriptive term of Time Method CV item in the local language.
Required: Mandatory if '.../d:TimeMethod/r:Description/r:Content' element is present
Usage: Language of the Time Dimension descriptive term. ISO 639-1 codes are strongly encouraged to be used.
Usage: Type of sampling procedure used for data collection. Controlled vocabulary includes a typology of sampling methods. Code value of the CV "DDI Sampling Procedure".
Usage: Use the string "DDI Sampling Procedure" regardless of language.
Usage: The Canonical URL of the CV version that is being used e.g. https://vocabularies.cessda.eu/v2/vocabularies/SamplingProcedure/2.0.1?languageVersion=en-2.0.1 .
Usage: Descriptive term of the Sampling Procedure CV item in the local language.
Required: Mandatory if '.../d:SamplingProcedure/r:Description/r:Content' element is present
Usage: Language of the Sampling Procedure Descriptive term. ISO 639-1 codes are strongly encouraged to be used.
Usage: Either a single point in time or start of a date range. Ideally YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ format, but can accept YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM or YYYY.
Usage: End of a date range. If data collection period is a single event, then this element is not required. Ideally YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ format, but can accept YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM or YYYY.
Usage: Use if data collection period is a single event. Ideally YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ format, but can accept YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM or YYYY.
Usage: This is the free text expression of the collection period. This element may be empty.
Required: Mandatory if '...Content' element is populated with a freetext expression.
Usage: Language of the free text expression of the collection period. ISO 639-1 codes are strongly encouraged to be used.
Usage: Description of the method of data collection - the procedure, technique, or mode of inquiry used to attain the data. Code value of the CV "DDI Mode of Data Collection".
Usage: Use the string "DDI Mode of Collection" regardless of language.
Usage: The Canonical URL of the CV version that is being used e.g. https://vocabularies.cessda.eu/v2/vocabularies/ModeOfCollection/5.0.0?languageVersion=en-5.0.0 .
Usage: Descriptive term of the Mode of Data Collection CV item in the local language.
Required: Mandatory if '.../d:ModeOfCollection/r:Description/r:Content' element is present
Usage: Language of the Mode of Data Collection descriptive term. ISO 639-1 codes are strongly encouraged to be used.

Usage: Controlled description of the data access (open access vs. restricted access). CDC will use the COAR Access Rights vocabulary https://vocabularies.coar-repositories.org/access_rights/. Only the codes "restricted access" and "open access" will be used.
Usage: When the AccessTypeName element is used for vocabulary 'COAR Access Right Vocabulary', then use this vocabulary name 'COAR Access Right Vocabulary' in this field.
Usage: Refers to any information relating to the access and use conditions of data that the SP wants to appear in the CDC.
Required: Mandatory if '..a:Access/r:Description/r:Content' element is present
Usage: Language of the Access statement. ISO 639-1 codes are strongly encouraged to be used.
Usage: The repeatable parent element for the Individual information.
Usage: Identifier of the Individual which is used when referencing this individual e.g. CreatorReference. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Identifier of the Individual which is used when referencing this individual e.g. CreatorReference. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Identifier of the Individual which is used when referencing this individual e.g. CreatorReference. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Identifier of the Individual which is used when referencing this individual e.g. CreatorReference. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: The full name of the Individual
Usage: The repeatable parent element for the Individual PID.
Usage: The type of the PID. If the PID is documented, the type of the PID should be documented, too.
Usage: The persistent identifier (PID) of the Individual
Usage: The URL of the PID.
Usage: The repeatable parent element for the Organization information.
Usage: Identifier of the Organization which is used when referencing this Organization e.g. CreatorReference. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Identifier of the Organization which is used when referencing this Organization e.g. CreatorReference. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Identifier of the Organization which is used when referencing this Organization e.g. CreatorReference. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Identifier of the Organization which is used when referencing this Organization e.g. CreatorReference. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: The name of the Organization
Usage: The URL of persistent identifier (PID) of the organization
Required: Mandatory if '//a:Organization/r:UserID' element is present.
Usage: The type of the PID. Please note, that the this attribute is mandatory for the UserID element due to the DDI-L specification.
Usage: The repeatable parent element for the Relation information.
Usage: Identifier of the Relation. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Identifier of the Relation. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Identifier of the Relation. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Identifier of the Relation. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: The parent element for the SourceObject information.
Usage: Reference information of the Individual. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Reference information of the Individual. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Reference information of the Individual. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Reference information of the Individual. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: The type of the referenced object, which in this case is always Individual
Usage: The parent element for the TargetObject information.
Usage: Reference information of the Organization. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Reference information of the Organization. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Reference information of the Organization. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: Reference information of the Organization. Either DDI URN or triple Agency, ID, Version (or both) need to be provided.
Usage: The type of the referenced object, which in this case is always Organization
Usage: The role of the TargetObject (Organization), which in this case should always be set as 'Affiliation'
